name: Microsoft Forms API (Graph API)
what_it_does: Microsoft 365 survey and form tool accessible via Microsoft Graph API — enabling Microsoft 365 organizations to create forms, collect responses, and integrate survey data with Microsoft ecosystems (Teams, SharePoint, Power Automate, Excel), with OAuth2 and service account authentication for enterprise automation.
use_cases: Fetching form responses via Microsoft Graph API for automated survey data processing
Integrating Microsoft Forms with Teams via Graph API for team feedback collection workflows
Exporting survey results via Microsoft Graph API to Excel/SharePoint for analysis
Automating form creation via Graph API for dynamic survey generation in M365 workflows
Building Power Automate flows via Graph API triggered by new form submissions
not_for: Public-facing surveys for non-Microsoft-365 respondents (external sharing limited)
Advanced survey logic (Microsoft Forms lacks complex branching vs Qualtrics/Typeform)
Teams not on Microsoft 365 (Forms is exclusively a Microsoft 365 product)
best_when: An organization on Microsoft 365 needs lightweight form/survey capabilities integrated with Teams, SharePoint, or Power Automate — without additional SaaS subscriptions.
avoid_when: You need advanced survey logic, external respondents without Microsoft accounts, or aren't on Microsoft 365.

auth.notes: OAuth2 via Microsoft Identity Platform (Azure AD). Authorization: Bearer {access_token}. Scopes: Forms.Read, Forms.ReadWrite, Forms.Read.All (admin). Application permissions for daemon (service account) access: Forms.Read.All. Delegated permissions for user context. Microsoft Graph SDK available for all major languages. Tenant ID required for multi-tenant apps. App registration in Azure AD required. Webhooks via Microsoft Graph change notifications (subscription-based).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Included with Microsoft 365 subscription; no standalone free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Included in Microsoft 365 Business Basic ($6/user/month) and above
pricing.notes: Microsoft Corporation (Redmond, WA). Microsoft Forms included in all Microsoft 365 plans. API access via Microsoft Graph API — no additional cost beyond M365 subscription. Limitations: 200 questions per form, 50,000 responses per form, 5MB file upload. External sharing: forms can be shared with non-M365 users but API access requires M365 account. Power Automate: primary integration path for no-code automation. Microsoft Copilot for Microsoft 365: Form analysis capabilities emerging. GDPR: EU data boundary for EU tenants. FedRAMP authorized for US government. Forms API in Microsoft Graph: limited compared to UI capabilities — not all features exposed via API.

performance.rate_limits: Microsoft Graph: 10,000 req/10 min per app per tenant; Forms-specific limits undocumented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Microsoft Graph API (not Forms-native API). Azure AD app registration required. Forms API subset of Graph. Microsoft 365 subscription required. FedRAMP/HIPAA compliant. EU data boundary for GDPR. 10K req/10min Graph rate limit.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET requests idempotent. Form reads idempotent by form ID. Response reads idempotent by response ID. Graph change notifications: implement idempotent webhook handlers.
known_agent_gotchas: Microsoft Graph API (not dedicated Forms API) — Forms is a subset of Graph at /v1.0/me/drive/root/.../forms
Azure AD app registration required — tenant admin consent for Forms.Read.All application permission
Forms API coverage is limited: not all UI features exposed via API (limited branching, scoring, etc.)
Included with M365 but API requires Azure AD app registration — separate Azure portal setup
Microsoft Graph rate limits: 10,000 req/10 min per app per tenant — may throttle bulk response fetching
External respondents: forms shareable publicly but API access requires M365 account context

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth2 via Azure AD with granular scopes. FedRAMP High authorized. HIPAA Business Associate Agreement available. SOC2/ISO27001 certified. GDPR: EU data boundary for EU tenants. Microsoft infrastructure — world-class security posture. Forms data may contain sensitive survey responses (PII). Application permissions (Forms.Read.All) require admin consent — principle of least privilege. Microsoft Graph SDK handles token lifecycle securely.
